docs.title: Welcome to BabaDB
docs.text:
BabaDB is an encrypted, asynchronous, file-based database designed for working seamlessly with Pydantic models.

This lightweight library helps you store and retrieve your data securely and efficiently, leveraging async/await patterns in Python.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Async interface for smooth integration
  • Encrypted storage for data security
  • Integration with Pydantic models for type safety and validation
  • Easy-to-use API inspired by SQLModel
  • Flexible logging options

Get started by visiting the Usage page.

docs.title: BabaEngine(filename: str, logging: Optional[bool] = None)
docs.text:
• filename: Path to the database file.
  • logging:
  • None (default) — logs print to terminal.
  • False — logging disabled.
  • True — logs saved to baba_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS.log.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• write(data: str) -> None Append a serialized data line to the database file.

  • read_all() -> list[str] Read all data lines from the database file.

  • clear() -> None Clear all data in the database file.

  • _log(level: str, message: str) -> None Internal logging method.

docs.title: BabaSession(engine: BabaEngine, model: Type[BaseModel])
docs.text:
• engine: Instance of BabaEngine.
  • model: Pydantic model class representing the data schema.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
• add(model_instance: BaseModel) -> None Add a new record to the database.

  • get_all() -> list[str] Get all raw data lines.

  • get_all_models() -> list[BaseModel] Get all records as model instances.

  • get_by_id(id: Any) -> Optional[BaseModel] Retrieve a record by its id field.

  • delete_by_id(id: Any) -> None Delete a record by its id field.

  • clear_cache() -> None Clear the session's internal cache.

docs.title: Baba(filename: str, logging: Optional[bool] = None, model: Type[BaseModel])
docs.text:
• High-level wrapper combining BabaEngine and BabaSession.
  • Provides convenient database operations with specified Pydantic model.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
```python db = Baba('products.baba', logging=True, model=Product) await db.session.add(Product(id=1, name="Apple", price=0.99)) all_products = await db.session.get_all_models() ````

For detailed examples, see the Usage page.

docs.title: Basic Example
docs.text:
import asyncio
from baba import Baba, BabaSession
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Product(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    price: float

async def main():
    db = Baba('products.baba', logging=True)

    async with BabaSession(db.engine, Product) as session:
        await session.add(Product(id=1, name="Apple", price=0.99))
        await session.add(Product(id=2, name="Banana", price=0.79))

        all_products = await session.get_all_models()
        for product in all_products:
            print(product)

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Logging Modes
docs.text:
• logging=None (default): logs printed to terminal
  • logging=False: logging disabled
  • logging=True: logs saved to baba_logs/YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS.log

For more advanced usage and API details, check the API Reference.
